versus hawkish signals from Japan so the

supportive of our China front loing
stimulus theme in that they signal
economic recovery struggling to achieve
escape velocity elsewhere the boj
announced its first plan reduction in
bond buying since late December the
central bank will buy 425 billion yen of
5 to 10 year jgbs down from the 476
billion of planned purchases last month
terms of Market implications although we
maintain that Beijing is pushing on a
string with respect to its policy
prescriptions specifically favoring
monetary stimulus over fiscal stimulus
we do believe that they will continue
pushing on sad string until economic
conditions materially improve that
outcome may be a long ways away is
evidenced by the first outright
contraction in total social financing
since October of 2005 in April elsewhere
the pullback in bond purchases
potentially marks a shift in the boj's L
Fair attitude towards Japan's collapsing
currency which is plunged in recent
months to the lowest exchange rate
versus the US dollar since the early
1990s the boj following through on the
shift could come at an awkward time for
asset markets from the perspective of
adding to Global bond market volatility
alongside the hawkish net financing
policy pivot by the the US Treasury
Department Q3 could be an especially
volatile quarter in this regard as

dashboard here wrapping up with the

through that so uh let me go through
each step by step here so inflation
indicators lag behind that is correct
inflation is the most lagging cycle
within the broader business cycle so uh
We've argued that the FED in response to
either sticky to a combination of sticky
inflation and and a significant
loosening of of of Labor slack in the
labor market we think they would cut
rates in that regard and that would be
the appropriate thing to do because the
employment cycle is a leading indicator
of the infation cycle U there are other
cycles that lead the employment cycle
but you have to understand the
progression of of Cycles within the
business cycle and inflation is the most
lacking of those so the FED is correct
to identify and be focused on employment
here in terms of guiding its uh guiding
its monetary policy and for guidance
here as opposed to inflation uh coupled
with slowing employment they predict the
FED uh will cut interest rates sooner
than expected for surprising markets I
accurate is his opinion uh we can't
ascertain how accurate that opinion is
uh you know from an accy perspective
because none of us uh knows the future
but it it does it's a reasonable thing
to expect if we have a significant
slowing of inl from here we're starting
to see very very starting to see some
leading indicators show a um a more
material loosening of Labor Market uh
conditionings uh so we have not seen a
preponderance of indicators uh support
that view but we are starting to see
some indicators support that view so in
our opinion I don't know that we are
have we are able as investors to have a
real high conviction view on that yet uh
we need some more data to uh to to to
adopt a different theme there uh and
then are there logical reasons
supporting the possibility of sudden uh
rate reductions uh the answer is um at
this current juncture no uh you know the
labor market is by and large remains
resilient uh based on the preponderance
of the latest data we do as I mentioned
are starting to see some indicators that
the labor market might be slowing at a
more uh more more expeditious Pace in in
a way that is negative for growth and if
that's the case if we start to see that
permeate across more uh uh indicators of
you know the balance of data then that
would support the possibility of a
sudden rate reduction so uh as always in
our opinion again if you're focusing
your entire investment process on
whether the FED Cuts rates in June or
July or September or November uh God
help you I have no idea what to say to
you but that's that's not a that's not
going to be a very effective solution uh
particularly when you get into 2025
because we're not going to be talking
about if they're going to cut rates in
June of July in 2025 so we would
recommend you find a more robust
defensible uh uh research and risk
management process to help guide your
portfolio through these highly uncertain
times I was on Fox Business last week
and we were talking with my with Iber
friend Charles Payne about how the
growth inflation and policy fundamentals
which were ubiquitously bullish from the
start of the bull run in November to
let's call it say mid-march have really
started to move in different directions
and from my perspective I think it's
very difficult even for someone as
sophisticated as myself to understand
where asset markets are how asset
markets are pric in those the changes in
those fundamentals uh in my opinion I
think it's really important to lean on
your risk management process uh
processes in in in confusing times like
this when the fundamental narrative is
less clear for asset markets and I can
tell you right now if the only thing
you're focused on is the Fed cutting
interest rates in June July August
September October November December I
can tell you you're probably not going
to make very much money trading asset
markets so uh definitely find somebody
who can help you with that expand your
investment process so wrap it up there
